summary: SANDWORM_MODE npm worm injects malicious MCP servers into AI coding tools
details: Self-replicating npm worm (evolution of Shai-Hulud) that steals npm/GitHub tokens, then publishes trojanised versions of every package the victim maintains. The SANDWORM_MODE phase (February 2026) added MCP server injection into AI coding tools including Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code Continue, and Windsurf. Injected MCP servers perform prompt injection to manipulate AI agents into exfiltrating credentials, harvesting LLM API keys, and SSH propagation to other systems.

agent_threat.attack_vector: Worm injects malicious MCP server configs into AI coding tools, manipulates AI agents via prompt injection to exfiltrate credentials
mitigations: Scan MCP servers with cybersecify before connecting
Use mcp-secure signTool() to pin tool definitions at registration
Verify tool definitions with verifyTool() before invocation
